title: HDC package config — Proxmox infrastructure
description: packages/infrastructure/proxmox/config.json — hypervisor clusters, provision defaults, optional bootstrap_hosts for users bootstrap-hdc.

id.description: Logical cluster id (matches inventory proxmox_cluster.id when applicable).

pve_release.description: Optional Proxmox VE release hint (e.g. 8.4 or 9) when API /version is unavailable; selects hdc API/CLI profile.

api_base.description: Deprecated: use web_ui on each host instead.

pve_node.description: Proxmox node name as shown in the API (often same as id).

web_ui.description: Proxmox web UI URL (https://host:8006) — used as API base.

ssh.description: SSH URL for bootstrap-hdc and remote ops (e.g. ssh://root@192.0.2.11).

down.description: When true, skip maintain/query/SSH iteration for this host; deploy targeting this id fails fast.

proxmox_cluster.description: Deprecated: cluster id is taken from clusters[].id.

local_lvm.description: Per-host local-LVM extend override and extra-disk RAID pools.

service_accounts.description: Declarative Proxmox users and API tokens for third-party consumers (e.g. homepage widget).

enabled.description: When true (default), maintain grows local-lvm when the boot disk has unallocated space after the PVE partition.

storage_id.description: Proxmox storage id for the default thin pool (default local-lvm).

enabled.description: When true (default), process clusters[].hosts[].local_lvm.pools on each node.

content.description: Default pvesm content for extra lvmthin pools (default images,rootdir).

vg.description: LVM volume group on the RAID device.

thin_pool.description: Thin pool logical volume name (default data).

md_name.description: Optional mdadm array name under /dev/md/ (defaults to vg with non-alnum replaced).

level.description: Linux MD RAID level (0 only for now).

devices.description: Block devices (prefer /dev/disk/by-id/...). One device for a direct PV pool; two or more for RAID0.

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips ensuring hdc API token role/ACL via SSH (default true).

role.description: Proxmox role id for the hdc API token (default HDCMaintain).

userid.description: Proxmox user that owns the hdc API token (default root@pam).

privileges.description: Privilege list for the role; defaults to hdc maintain set (VM.Audit, Datastore.Audit, …).

id.description: Stable reference for other packages (e.g. homepage uses service_account_id: homepage).

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips this account (default true).

userid.description: Proxmox user id (name@realm), created via pveum user add when missing.

tokenid.description: API token id under the user (privsep token).

role.description: Proxmox role for token ACL at / (default PVEAuditor).

privileges.description: Custom role privileges when role is not a built-in Proxmox role.

password_vault_key.description: Vault env var name for the user password (auto-generated on first maintain).

token_vault_key.description: Vault env var name for the API token (user@realm!tokenid=secret).

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips installing local ~/.ssh public keys on hypervisors (default true).
When false, maintain skips no-subscription APT sources and subscription nag (default true).

disable_ceph_enterprise.description: Disable Ceph enterprise apt entries; does not add Ceph no-subscription (default true).

remove_subscription_nag.description: Install nag-removal script and apt Post-Invoke hook (default true).

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips restricting host SSH and web UI (port 8006) to allowed_source_cidrs (default true).

allowed_source_cidrs.description: Source networks allowed for SSH and Proxmox web UI (default example RFC5737 subnets; gateway-style x.x.x.1/24 normalized to .0/24 on apply).

web_port.description: Proxmox web UI port to restrict (default 8006).

enabled.description: When true, maintain writes /etc/pve/firewall/<vmid>.fw for vmids from service package deployments.

allowed_source_cidrs.description: Inbound allow list for managed guests (falls back to host_firewall cidrs when omitted).

manage_from_deployments.description: When true (default), collect vmids from packages/services/*/config.json deployments.

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips NAS storage ensure (default true).

ids.description: Storage ids to ensure (default nas-a, nas-b).

discover_from_host.description: Inventory host id to prefer when discovering storage definitions (e.g. hypervisor-a).

targets.description: Explicit storage definitions (Proxmox storage.cfg fields). When set, used instead of API discovery.

schedule.description: Proxmox calendar schedule (e.g. sun 03:00, daily, hourly).

prune_backups.description: Retention string for API prune-backups (e.g. keep-last=3).

storage.description: Optional storage override for this profile.

order.description: Proxmox startup order (lower starts first; same order starts together).

up.description: Seconds to wait after this order group before starting the next (default from provision.startup.default_up).

down.description: Optional shutdown order delay (Proxmox startup down=).

rate.description: Optional replication rate limit in MB/s.

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips replication job ensure (default true).

default_profile.description: Profile name for guests without a service replication override (default frequent).

manage_from_deployments.description: When true (default), scan service deployments for replication targets.

nodes.description: Inventory hypervisor ids (host_id) allowed for this HA group.

restricted.description: When true, resources in this group may only run on listed nodes.

nofailback.description: When true, do not fail back to the original node after recovery.

group.description: Default HA group id for managed resources.

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips HA group/resource ensure (default true).

manage_from_deployments.description: When true (default), scan service deployments for HA resources.

groups.description: Named HA groups (id → nodes and options).

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips guest startup order ensure (default true).

default_up.description: Default up= delay in seconds between startup order groups (default 30).

manage_from_deployments.description: When true (default), scan service deployments and apply startup order from priorities or per-guest startup.

priorities.description: Package id to startup order map (e.g. bind: 1, nginx-waf: 2, postfix-relay: 3).

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips backup job ensure (default true).

default_storage.description: Default backup destination storage id (e.g. nas-1).

default_profile.description: Profile name for guests without a service backup override (default weekly).

manage_from_deployments.description: When true (default), scan service deployments for backup targets.

job_id_prefix.description: Prefix for hdc-managed backup job ids (default hdc-backup).

profiles.description: Named backup schedules and retention policies.

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips notification target/matcher ensure and does not set backup job notification-mode (default true when mailto is set).

mailto.description: Operator email for backup failure notifications (Sendmail target).

sendmail_target.description: Proxmox sendmail notification target id (default hdc-mail).

backup_failure_matcher.description: Matcher id routing vzdump error events to sendmail_target (default hdc-backup-failures).

disable_matchers.description: Additional matcher ids to disable (e.g. default catch-all matchers).

disable_legacy_backup_success_matchers.description: When true (default), disable matchers that would notify on successful backup (vzdump info) events.

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips apt update/upgrade on hypervisors (default true).

reboot_wait_seconds.description: Max seconds to wait for SSH after each sequential reboot (default 300).

enabled.description: When false, maintain skips reporting ACPI SLIC/MSDM OEM Windows license tables and QEMU -acpitable passthrough (default true).

policy.description: ubuntu-lts (default): sync all supported Ubuntu LTS LXC vztmpl and QEMU templates; prune older Ubuntu templates.

ostemplate_storage.description: Storage for CT templates (vztmpl), e.g. local.

default_release.description: Ubuntu LTS release for default ostemplate (e.g. 22.04).

storage.description: Disk storage for VM and cloud-init (e.g. local-lvm).

image_storage.description: Directory storage for downloaded cloud .img before import (e.g. local).

default_release.description: Ubuntu LTS release for default QEMU template (e.g. 22.04 → vmid 9022).

template_vmid.description: Default QEMU template vmid for create-vm (catalog: 9022=22.04, 9024=24.04, 9026=26.04).

template_name.description: Guest name before conversion to template.

build_template.description: When true (default), maintain builds template_vmid from cloud_image_url if missing.

cloud_image_url.description: HTTP(S) URL of cloud image (.img) for QEMU template build.

settle_seconds.description: Delay after QEMU start before SSH probe on fresh clone deploy (default 45).

ssh_probe_seconds.description: Short SSH wait before optional Proxmox reboot workaround (default 90).

ssh_timeout_seconds.description: Full SSH wait after reboot workaround (default 300).

reboot_on_probe_fail.description: When true (default), reboot guest via Proxmox API if short SSH probe fails (serial-console hang).
